elements.heading: Agreements in Competition
elements.htmlContent:
1. When they dbl our lr bid; xx by us = accept inv; eg 1m2m(x)xx = gf

  2. In competition, we spl only in the enemy suit; except in the following cases

  • a cue raise, followed by a jump in ns = spl

  • when < 2 new suits available for bidding naturally, jumps in ns = spl. Eg: …

  1. When opps have bid 1 suit then bidding/ raising that suit = Michaels (1x)2x. This also applies when they bid via a transfer: eg: (1n)p(2♦)?, x = ♦ suit, 2♥ = Michaels

  2. If opps bid 2 suits, say (1♣)p(1♥)?, bidding responder’s suit (2♥)  = nat; opener’s suit (2♣) = Michaels. Same when the 2nd suit is bid via a xfer; eg (1♣)p(1♥ = 4+♠)? x = ♥; 1♠ = ♠ suit; 2♣ = Michaels

  3. 3 level (or higher, non jump) fs bid by PH = LR in opener’s 2nd suit, fs is artificial; same for 2 level when the bid is not a Gazilli/ xyz bid: Eg. p1♦1♠2♣2♥ = LR in ♣

  4. When opps interfere over our art bids (below game level), based on context: x /xx = forward going, bal/ stolen bid; pass = worry / unable to make normal bid; rest – as if no interference

    • Interference over our 1n/2n/2♦: opener to pass w/o stop if below committed level

    • 1♠3♣(3♥): x = ♥ sing;  pass = bal (would have bid 3♦); rest same

    • 1♥1n2♣(x): xx = 8+, pass = < 8

  5. When we make a bid (after they open) of undefined srength (x, 2 suiter split range overcalls), subsequent cue bid shows strength, bidding ns or rebidding our suits shows shape

  6. In competition, all jumps to 4M = to play

  7. When opps have made a forcing bid say 1d(1h)x(3d)? pass and pull = compete; direct bid = strength

elements.heading: Consulting partner on 5 level competition
elements.htmlContent:
1. Cue asuggesting sarifice: Lho opens, p overcalls, rho makes a value bid: (1♠)3♣(x)3♠; (1♠)2♣(3♣)3♠; Now 3 level cue or higher by advancer = suggest sacrifice at one level > their game level

  2. Fit Double Jump/ 4 level single jump: Jumps at 4 level in competition after we bid/ overcall M: eg 1♥(x)4♦, 1♥(1♠)4♦, shows big fit and playing strength (typically 1 high card each in both suits) to enable 5 level decision

    • generally weakish, eg 1♥(3♣)? Jx xxxxx Axxx Ax, bid 4♦ (direct 4♥ denies any outside cards)

    • but (1♥)1♠(2♠)? 4♦ is also the right bid when there is no other bid to show values + raise - kxxx xx akjxx xx

  3. After we have found fit, new suit bid at the 4 level, (or 3 level > 3 of trumps) = playing strength bid ( with strength, double). Eg 1h(2c)2h(3c)3s = values in spades (partner can look at spades to consider 5 level competition)

elements.htmlContent:
Cue rebid by opener = one of the following

  • strong own suit/ support for partner/ stopper ask: Cue followed by own suit bid/p's suit / ns, establishes gf. eg: 1♥(1♠)x(2♠)3♠; 1♦(1♥)1♠(2♥)3♥

  • Unassuming cue: cue when p’s bid promises a rebid: Eg: 1♥(1♠)2♦(p)2♠: the cue bid can be a “forced” bid: (if p now makes an nf bid I can pass),

  • When we have found fit, cue is simply game try or finding suitable game (nt or suit). so 1h(2d)2h(p)3d = help ask in d (have long d, need help)

1. When they dbl our lr bid; xx by us = accept inv; eg 1m2m(x)xx = gf

  2. In competition, we spl only in the enemy suit; except in the following cases

  • a cue raise, followed by a jump in ns = spl

  • when < 2 new suits available for bidding naturally, jumps in ns = spl. Eg: …

  1. When opps have bid 1 suit then bidding/ raising that suit = Michaels (1x)2x. This also applies when they bid via a transfer: eg: (1n)p(2♦)?, x = ♦ suit, 2♥ = Michaels

  2. If opps bid 2 suits, say (1♣)p(1♥)?, bidding responder’s suit (2♥)  = nat; opener’s suit (2♣) = Michaels. Same when the 2nd suit is bid via a xfer; eg (1♣)p(1♥ = 4+♠)? x = ♥; 1♠ = ♠ suit; 2♣ = Michaels

  3. 3 level (or higher, non jump) fs bid by PH = LR in opener’s 2nd suit, fs is artificial; same for 2 level when the bid is not a Gazilli/ xyz bid: Eg. p1♦1♠2♣2♥ = LR in ♣

  4. When opps interfere over our art bids (below game level), based on context: x /xx = forward going, bal/ stolen bid; pass = worry / unable to make normal bid; rest – as if no interference

    • Interference over our 1n/2n/2♦: opener to pass w/o stop if below committed level

    • 1♠3♣(3♥): x = ♥ sing;  pass = bal (would have bid 3♦); rest same

    • 1♥1n2♣(x): xx = 8+, pass = < 8

  5. When we make a bid (after they open) of undefined srength (x, 2 suiter split range overcalls), subsequent cue bid shows strength, bidding ns or rebidding our suits shows shape

  6. In competition, all jumps to 4M = to play

  7. In competition, when we make a bid (after they open) of undefined srength (x, 2 suiter split range overcalls), subsequent cue bid shows strength, bidding ns or rebidding our suits shows shape

  8. In competition, all 4M jumps = to play

  9. When opps have made a forcing bid say 1d(1h)x(3d)?, pass and pull = compete; direct bid = strength

  10. In competition, avoid strength showing double, when holding extra shape/ eg akxx akqxxxx x x -1h(1S)-2H-(3m) Don’t x 3m- make any F bid like 3s/4m

elements.htmlContent:
Basic form: 1x(1y)p(p)?. or (1x)p(p)?. What are the considerations for pass vs taking action?

In favour

  1. We may make a part score contract and 1y may make

  2. We may get a penalty in (1y)-X

  3. we may even make a game, but 3N may not be the right game

  4. We may get a penalty. Considerations that favour penalty - 1. Short in partner suit ( upto XX);2. Lower cards in trumps taking tricks ;3. Values outside ;4. Vulnerability

Not in favour

  1. They may find an alternate ( profitable) part score contract (rho may have passed his partner ♠ 1♠ on x jxxxxx qxxxx x and a red suit fit may exist);

  2. We may push them to a game

  3. we may overreach, converting plus to minus

  4. Partner may err in auction due to the wide range scope of your balancing bid.

  5. You may be competing against 3y ( I e 1y may be making 2 OTs), not realising your bids at 2/3 level are competing “under”

  6. Reopening in balancing seat and defending their contract later is like making a 2 suited overcall and defending later - I e you have (1)taken the risk ;(2) given information - both with nil return( if their contract makes
